name: x-browser-automation-playbook
description: Use this skill for interactive work on X.com — posting, replying, liking, searching, collecting tweets, opening bookmarks, and other live browser actions. For plain X status URLs that the user wants fetched or read, default to `web_fetch` for one URL or `web_fetch_batch` for several URLs and answer from those results unless the user explicitly asks for more. Triggers on requests like post on X, tweet this, reply to this tweet, like tweets, search X, collect tweets, find my X bookmarks, open X bookmarks, go to bookmarks, fetch this X post, read this tweet URL, pull this X thread, or webfetch this X URL.

resources.description: Troubleshooting and successful-run example for the read-only Daily X Signal Radar collector, including the 2026-05-17 text-first success after no-activity failures.
Read-only on-demand X home-feed/vibe-check example for loose “go on X and tell me what’s happening” requests.
Read-only competitor feature scan workflow for X: collect latest competitor feature posts and translate them into Prometheus product implications, with snapshot-error fallback.
Troubleshooting example for X browser automation when Chrome debug profile/port 9222 blocks first open attempts but a later explicit retry may succeed.
Example recovery flow for using Grok through X OAuth when anonymous Grok blocks a file-upload release-thread drafting task.
